FSM_REQ Body

Package to implement the FSM related logic for requests

Author

Juergen Sieben, ConDeS GmbH

Helper-Methods
persist
procedure persist(
p_req 
in out nocopy fsm_req_type
)

Method to persist an instance or changes for an instance

Parameter
p_req
in out nocopy fsm_req_type

Instance of FSM_REQ_TYPE to persist

Event-Handlers
raise_default
function raise_default(
p_req 
in out nocopy fsm_req_type,
p_fev_id 
in varchar2
)
return binary_integer

Generic event handler, just proceeds to the next possible event.

Parameters
p_req
in out nocopy fsm_req_type

Instance of FSM_REQ_TYPE

p_fev_id
in varchar2

Event to throw

Returns

One of the constants FSM.C_OK or FSM.C_ERROR indicating whether the event could be raised successfully

Throws
msg.FSM_NEXT_STATUS_NU_ERR

if more than one next status is possible.  This error must be circumvented by creating a separate event handler which deterministically decides on the next status

raise_check
function raise_check(
p_req 
in out nocopy fsm_req_type
)
return binary_integer

Decides how the decision on granting rights must be made.

Parameter
p_req
in out nocopy fsm_req_type

Instance of FSM_REQ_TYPE

Returns

One of the constants FSM.C_OK or FSM.C_ERROR indicating whether the event could be raised successfully

Throws
msg.FSM_NEXT_STATUS_NU_ERR

if more than one next status is possible.  This error must be circumvented by creating a separate event handler which deterministically decides on the next status

raise_nil
function raise_nil(
p_req 
in out nocopy fsm_req_type
)
return binary_integer

Does nothing.

Parameter
p_req
in out nocopy fsm_req_type

Instance of FSM_REQ_TYPE

Returns

One of the constants FSM.C_OK or FSM.C_ERROR indicating whether the event could be raised successfully

Throws
msg.FSM_NEXT_STATUS_NU_ERR

if more than one next status is possible.  This error must be circumvented by creating a separate event handler which deterministically decides on the next status

Interface
create_fsm_req
procedure create_fsm_req(
p_req 
in out nocopy fsm_req_type,
p_req_id 
in fsm_requests.req_id%type
 default 
null,
p_req_fst_id 
in fsm_status_v.fst_id%type
 default 
fsm_fst.REQ_CREATED,
p_req_fev_list 
in fsm_events_v.fev_id%type
 default 
null,
p_req_rtp_id 
in fsm_request_types.rtp_id%type,
p_req_rre_id 
in fsm_requestors.rre_id%type,
p_req_text 
in fsm_requests.req_text%type
)

See FSM_REQ.create_fsm_req

create_fsm_req
procedure create_fsm_req(
p_req 
in out nocopy fsm_req_type,
p_req_id 
in fsm_requests.req_id%type
)

See FSM_REQ.create_fsm_req

persist_request
procedure persist_request(
p_req_id 
in number,
p_req_rtp_id 
in varchar2,
p_req_rre_id 
in varchar2,
p_req_text 
in varchar2
)

See FSM_REQ.persist_request

raise_event
function raise_event(
p_req 
in out nocopy fsm_req_type,
p_fev_id 
in fsm_events_v.fev_id%type
)
return binary_integer

See FSM_REQ.raise_event

raise_event
procedure raise_event(
p_req_id 
in fsm_objects_v.fsm_id%type,
p_fev_id 
in fsm_events_v.fev_id%type
)

See FSM_REQ.raise_event

set_status
function set_status(
p_req 
in out nocopy fsm_req_type
)
return binary_integer

See FSM_REQ.set_status
